threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the bus' instead of 'the 38' or whatever. Local kids can name every route by heart, and you just stare.
Caught the 36 last night; the driver serenaded us with 90s R&B. Everyone sang along, even the usually grumpy bloke in the back.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I spotted an elderly man feeding the ducks at Hermannplatz while discussing politics with a group of teenagers.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner kebab stand serving vegan 'fleisch' at 3 AM, a testament to the city’s late-night culinary creativity.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to cling to the handrail on BART when it's stopped—those sudden jerks make tourists stumble like they've never ridden the subway.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today stopping at every corner, but the driver took a moment to give directions to a lost tourist. Classic SF, where kindness slips through the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars in Gràcia are trendy; try La Xampanyeria on a weekday and see locals just enjoying their canyó de xampany.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confusion over the differences between 'bar' and 'casa de comidas'—the locals know where to find the best tapas.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a cuppa and say 'thank you' to the barista, but they just stare blankly unless you add a quirky name for it.
Last night on the 59, a woman serenaded us with 'Ain't Nobody' while her mate tried to beatbox; even the driver laughed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit casually buying döner and sipping on an espresso while waiting for the tram. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do they have a currywurst stand that opens at 3 AM, serving hungry clubbers fresh sausage smothered in curry ketchup. It's a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name three different burrito places but still complain about BART’s 30,000 delays like it’s part of the city’s charm.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a guy serenaded passengers with a saxophone on the 49. A perfect mix of joy and chaos—only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they’re being cute with Spanish, but locals roll their eyes when they order tapas with ‘tah-pahs’. It’s ‘tah-pahs’, not a food pun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their excitement at ordering 'café con leche' without a hesitation, while locals stick to 'café amb llet' like it's second nature.
